_meta.note: Sourced historical + present-day facts for the first-principles deep dives. Computed math (issuance, S2F, purchasing power, denarius decay) lives in first-principles-math.json. Source tiers follow the deep-dive framework: T1 primary/official, T2 reputable secondary/academic, T3 journalism, T4 community-tracked/estimate.
_meta.live_data_note: Present-day figures (BTC supply, node count, price) are bound via data-btc-live where possible; hardcoded fallbacks below are dated and labelled.

present_day_anchors.reachable_nodes_caveat: Bitnodes counts LISTENING/reachable nodes only (~21k-24k through 2026); total validating nodes including non-listening are materially higher but not directly countable. Crawler methodology shifted mid-2026.
present_day_anchors.network_hashrate_caveat: Bitcoin network hashrate fluctuated ~888-937 EH/s across June 2026 (it is inferred from block times + difficulty, not measured directly, so trackers differ). Used as the default + slider midpoint for the miner-economics and 51%-attack calculators in original-question-everything.html. Illustrative, not a quote; update on re-audit.

sources.label: Bitcoin Core, controlled supply / subsidy schedule
CoinWarz Bitcoin Hashrate Chart (~934 EH/s, Jun 2026)

debasement_rome.claim: The Roman silver denarius fell from ~95% silver under Augustus to ~2-5% by the late 3rd century (Aurelian).

sources.label: Kenneth W. Harl, Coinage in the Roman Economy, 300 B.C. to A.D. 700 (Johns Hopkins UP, 1996)

debasement_rome.caveat: Per-emperor fineness figures are numismatic estimates with a few-percent error band; the trend (steep multi-century decline) is not in dispute, the exact per-year values are approximate.

_source_primary.label: Hanke & Krus, 'World Hyperinflations' (Cato Working Paper / Routledge Handbook of Major Events in Economic History, 2013), the canonical academic table

episodes.note: The highest monthly inflation rate ever recorded. Currency replaced by the forint, Aug 1946.
Currency abandoned in 2009 in favour of foreign currencies.
Driven by money-financing of deficits and reparations. Ended with the Rentenmark, Nov 1923.

episodes.annual_inflation_note: IMF projected ~1,000,000% for 2018; cumulative collapse of the bolívar's value.
episodes.migration: ~7.7 million Venezuelans had left the country by the mid-2020s (R4V/UNHCR).

sources.label: IMF, Outlook for the Americas (2018 1,000,000% projection)
R4V (Inter-Agency Coordination Platform for Refugees and Migrants from Venezuela)

confiscation_and_censorship.title: US gold confiscation (1933–34)
confiscation_and_censorship.claim: Executive Order 6102 (Apr 5, 1933) required citizens to deliver most gold to the Federal Reserve at $20.67/oz; the Gold Reserve Act (Jan 1934) then revalued gold to $35/oz.
confiscation_and_censorship.implication: Citizens who complied were paid $20.67/oz; the dollar was then devalued ~41% against gold ($20.67→$35).

sources.label: Gold Reserve Act of 1934, Federal Reserve History

confiscation_and_censorship.title: Cyprus deposit bail-in (2013)
confiscation_and_censorship.claim: In the 2013 rescue, uninsured deposits (over €100,000) at Bank of Cyprus were converted to equity; the haircut on uninsured deposits reached roughly 47.5%.
confiscation_and_censorship.caveat: Insured deposits (≤€100,000) were protected. The ~47.5% figure applies to uninsured deposits at Bank of Cyprus specifically.

sources.label: Reuters, Cyprus bank depositors to lose up to 47.5% (2013)

confiscation_and_censorship.title: India demonetisation (2016)
confiscation_and_censorship.claim: On Nov 8, 2016, India withdrew ₹500 and ₹1000 notes, about 86% of the value of currency in circulation, as legal tender.
confiscation_and_censorship.caveat: 86% refers to value of notes in circulation, not 86% of cash transactions or money supply. The RBI later reported ~99% of demonetised notes were returned.

sources.label: Reserve Bank of India, Annual Report 2016–17 (demonetisation)

confiscation_and_censorship.title: Canada Emergencies Act account freezes (2022)
confiscation_and_censorship.claim: In Feb 2022 the federal government invoked the Emergencies Act; under the Emergency Economic Measures Order, banks froze accounts tied to the protests, the Public Order Emergency Commission documented roughly 280 accounts (~CA$8M) frozen.
confiscation_and_censorship.caveat: The earlier page claim that '$50 donors had accounts frozen' is anecdotal/contested. Use the documented POEC figures. A Federal Court later ruled (2024) the invocation was unreasonable and unconstitutional, under appeal.

sources.label: Public Order Emergency Commission (Rouleau Commission) Final Report
Federal Court of Canada 2024 ruling (Canadian Frontline Nurses v. Canada)

bitcoin_resilience.uptime_claim: Bitcoin's base network has had two notable consensus incidents, the Aug 2010 value-overflow bug (block 74638) and the Mar 2013 v0.7/v0.8 chain split, both resolved within hours, both in the first four years. None since 2013.
bitcoin_resilience.uptime_pct_estimate: ~99.98% (estimate; framed as 'two brief early incidents, none since 2013')

stock_to_flow_model_critique.claim: The PlanB Stock-to-Flow PRICE model is excluded from these pages as a predictor because it has been falsified out-of-sample.
stock_to_flow_model_critique.evidence: BTC price diverged from the S2F model by well over 500% across 2024; PlanB's $100k–$1M 'by end of 2021' and ~$500k 'by 2025' bands did not materialise. The model conflates a scarcity ratio (a real, computable property) with a price forecast (which it is not).
stock_to_flow_model_critique.framing_rule: On-page: present S2F ONLY as a scarcity descriptor (stock ÷ annual flow). Never imply 'high S2F → price must rise'. Always pair with the divergence-from-price evidence.

sources.label: Investopedia / multiple analyses documenting S2F price-model divergence post-2021
